recommended_ready_live_addition: Add /health/ready and /health/live probes. /live should return 200 with {'status':'alive'} if the event loop responds. /ready should return 200 only when engine_pool is initialized, at least one model is loaded OR an MCP manager is connected, and default_model is set; otherwise 503. Currently /health conflates liveness and readiness, has no auth gate (intentional for orchestrators), and never reports unhealthy, so K8s/load-balancer probes cannot distinguish a degraded process from a healthy one. Keep /health as a public liveness alias for backward compatibility, but route readiness decisions through /health/ready.
